learned_patterns.description: Always define the eval harness and golden dataset before writing the first prompt — this prevents 'prompt drift' where quality looks good but actually regressed
learned_patterns.notes: The eval harness IS the definition of done. Without it, prompt quality is subjective.

learned_patterns.description: Token budgets must be set before any LLM call reaches production. Use max_tokens cap always. Default caps: Haiku=500, Sonnet=2000, Opus=4000 (adjust per feature needs)
learned_patterns.notes: Unbounded LLM calls have caused runaway costs on other products. Cap everything.

learned_patterns.description: PII firewall: never send name+email together to external LLMs. Strip before calling. Log what categories were sent (not values) for audit.
learned_patterns.notes: Regulatory requirement for all products targeting EU/GCC users.

common_mistakes.mistake: Inlining prompts as string literals in code
common_mistakes.prevention: Always version prompts in .prompts/v1/[feature].txt files. Inline strings can't be versioned, rolled back, or A/B tested.

common_mistakes.mistake: Calling real LLM APIs in unit tests
common_mistakes.prevention: Unit tests must mock LLM calls with recorded fixtures. Only eval harnesses (AI_EVAL_MODE=1) call real APIs.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing fallback when LLM API is unavailable
common_mistakes.prevention: Every AI feature must have a fallback path. Catch errors, log them, return safe default.

preferred_approaches.approach: Use claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 with structured output (JSON mode). Define category enum in prompt. max_tokens=100.
Use claude-sonnet-4-6 with streaming. Cache results by input hash (Redis, TTL 1h) to avoid duplicate calls.
Use claude-opus-4-6. Always require ADR justifying cost. Document expected tokens per call.
